scope: Breadth-first pass over 2020-2025 changes, PLUS rules/exemptions for existing DB jurisdictions (which are 83% rule-less). Deltas vs. prod verified via live SQL lookup.
methodology: ~20 WebSearches split across thematic (age exemptions / stay caps / student / business traveler / diplomatic / long-stay), regional breadth (Eastern Europe, Baltics, UK cities, more US cities, Pacific, Caribbean, Middle East, Africa, South America), and historical backfill (2020-2023 changes).
summary: 28 new rate entries (historical and newly-discovered 2020-2025 changes) + 25 new rules/exemptions for jurisdictions that had rates but zero rules. Rule patterns heavily recur: under-X age exemption (X=10..18), N-night stay caps (7/10/21/30), permanent-resident long-stay exemption (30/90/180 days), business-traveler exemption in select cities.

findings.summary: Riga introduced €1/person/night tourist fee from Jan 2023. Max €10 (=10 nights cap). <18 exempt.

rates.source_quote: Starting in January 2023, Riga introduced a tourist fee of 1 euro per night per visitor for overnight stays, with a maximum charged amount of 10 euros. Children under the age of 18 will not be taxed.

rules.name: Under 18 exempt
rules.source_quote: Children under the age of 18 will not be taxed.

rules.name: 10-night cap
rules.source_quote: with a maximum charged amount of 10 euros.

findings.summary: Voluntary 'city visitor charge' £1/room/night from April 2023 via Accommodation BID model.

rates.source_quote: Manchester introduced a voluntary 'city visitor charge' in April 2023 through a partnership in which local businesses fund shared projects, with hotels in the city center charging guests $1.33 per room per night.

findings.summary: Liverpool ABID levy £2/night from June 2025 (~100 participating hotels).

rates.source_quote: Liverpool introduced its own ABID levy in June 2025, with approximately 100 participating hotels charging guests £2 per night.

findings.summary: Previously written under GB-SCT-EDH (which doesn't exist in DB). Correct code per DB is GB-SCT-EDI. 5% visitor levy from July 24, 2026; max 5 nights, pre-VAT.
Nashville-Davidson County local occupancy tax raised to 7% + $2.50/night from July 1, 2023 (+1% increase).

rates.source_quote: Nashville increased its local hotel occupancy tax after legislation was adopted in late 2022, with an additional 1% beginning collection on July 1, 2023, lifting the local occupancy tax rate to 7% while keeping a $2.50 nightly fee.

findings.summary: Seattle citywide hotel tax 15.7% + additional 2.3% on large downtown hotels.

rates.source_quote: The citywide hotel tax in Seattle is 15.7 percent, with an additional 2.3 percent imposed on large hotels downtown.
rates.notes: This is a combined stack; needs decomposition for proper modeling.

findings.summary: Portland city occupancy 6% + TID 3% + Multnomah County 5.5% = combined ~16% with state.
DC transient accommodation sales tax 15.95% from April 2023 through Sept 30, 2027 (includes temporary 1% addition).

rates.authority_name: DC Office of Tax and Revenue (OTR)
rates.source_quote: Washington DC now charges 15.95% through 30 September 2027.

findings.summary: $1.50 USD/person/night city tax for non-Argentine-resident guests since March 1, 2020. PLUS 21% VAT refund for foreign tourists paying with foreign cards (active program through NYE 2025).

rates.source_quote: Starting March 1, 2020, guests with permanent residency outside of Argentina are charged a city tax of USD 1.50 per person per night, not included in the rate.

rules.name: Argentine residents exempt
rules.description: Tax only applies to guests with permanent residency outside Argentina (non-residents).

findings.summary: Siem Reap hotel charges: 10% VAT + 2% city tax + 5% service charge on accommodation.
Vancouver: 8% PST on short-term accommodation + up to 3% Municipal & Regional District Tax (MRDT) for tourism marketing.
NEW: BHD 3/room/night tourist levy effective May 1, 2024.

rates.source_quote: Bahrain introduced a BHD 3 per room per day tourist levy on hotel accommodations, effective from May 1, 2024.

findings.summary: Fiji VAT cut 15%→12.5% on hotel accommodation from Aug 1, 2025. Departure tax phased: $170 Aug 2024, returning to $200 Aug 2025.

rates.source_quote: from August 1st 2025, the Fijian Government reduced its Value Added Tax (VAT) from a standard 15% down to 12.5%.

findings.summary: Tunisia hotel tourist tax from Nov 1, 2024: 2* hotels 4 TND, 3* 8 TND, 4*/5* 12 TND per person per night. Age 12+, first 10 nights only.

rates.source_quote: As of November 1, 2024, Tunisia increased its hotel tourist tax rates to 12 Tunisian dinars (TND) per person per night for stays in 4- and 5-star hotels and 8 TND per person per night for stays in 3-star hotels.

findings.summary: Taiwan hotel charges: 5% VAT + 10% customary service charge.
Puerto Rico Room Occupancy Tax: 9% general, 11% casino hotels, 7% inns / short-term supplemental, 5% all-inclusive.

rates.source_quote: The general tax rate for room occupancy is 9%, with higher rates for casino hotels at 11%, and lower rates for inns at 7% and supplementary short-term lodging at 7%. For all-inclusive hotels, the tax is 5% of the global charge.

rules.name: Casino hotels 11%
rules.description: Casino hotels pay 11% instead of the 9% base
rules.notes: property_type value `casino_hotel` not in our existing taxonomy; may need category extension.

findings.summary: Existing Milan tourist tax — add under-18 exemption rule (was missing).

rules.name: Under 18 exempt
rules.source_quote: An extra fee will not be applied to children under 18 years old.

findings.summary: Venice overnight contributo di soggiorno — add <14 exemption + 5-night cap rules.

rules.name: Under 14 exempt
rules.source_quote: Children under 14, Venice residents, students enrolled in local institutions, and those visiting for work are also exempt.

findings.summary: Rome contributo di soggiorno — add 10-night cap (was noted in prior research but may not have seeded).
Berlin 21-day cap on CityTax — documented but not in DB.

rules.name: 21-day consecutive cap
rules.source_quote: In Berlin, the tax is capped at 21 successive days.

findings.summary: NYC 180-day permanent-resident exemption (local sales tax).

rules.name: Permanent resident (180+ days)
rules.description: NYC local sales tax exempt for stays ≥ 180 consecutive days
rules.source_quote: However, in New York City, the local sales tax applies until a guest has stayed for at least 180 consecutive days.

findings.summary: NY State 90-day permanent-resident exemption.

rules.name: Permanent resident (90+ days)
rules.description: NY State tax exempt for stays ≥ 90 consecutive days

findings.summary: Texas 30-day permanent-resident exemption.

rules.name: Permanent resident (30+ days)
rules.description: Texas hotel occupancy tax exempt for stays ≥ 30 consecutive days
rules.source_quote: Guests who occupy a hotel room for 30 or more consecutive days are considered permanent residents and are exempt from hotel tax.

findings.summary: France national: under-18 exempt from taxe de séjour.

rules.name: Under 18 exempt (France national rule)
rules.source_quote: France: Those 18 years and under do not have to pay the tax.

findings.summary: Czech Republic: under-18 AND over-70 exempt from tourist tax.

rules.name: Over 70 exempt
rules.source_quote: People under 18 and over 70 are exempt from paying the tax.

findings.summary: Slovenia: <7 exempt, 7-18 half rate.

rules.name: Ages 7-18 half rate
rules.source_quote: Generally, children under seven are exempt and children aged between seven and eighteen are half the rate.

findings.summary: Austria national: students/apprentices staying for educational purposes may be exempt.

rules.name: Students/apprentices educational stay
rules.description: Guests in educational programs (students, pupils, apprentices) may be exempt
rules.source_quote: In Austria, people staying in a municipality for educational purposes, such as pupils, students or apprentices, may be exempt from the visitor's tax.

findings.summary: Barcelona <17 exempt from IEET / municipal surcharge.

rules.name: Under 17 exempt
rules.source_quote: In Barcelona, children under 17 are exempt.

findings.summary: Lisbon 7-night cap on Taxa Turística.
Boston: federal diplomatic and government-official exemption applies.

rules.name: Federal diplomatic/official exempt
rules.description: US diplomatic cardholders and US federal officials on business exempt per Mass.gov Directive 03-4

age_exemptions.description: Very common pattern — under X exempt, sometimes tiered 7-18 half-rate. Strong coverage in this file.

stay_length_caps.description: Cap on number of taxable nights. Typical values: 5, 7, 10, 21, 30.

permanent_resident_long_stay.description: US pattern — stays of X consecutive days make you a 'permanent resident' and thus exempt from hotel/occupancy tax.

examples: Austria: students / apprentices on educational stay exempt
Cologne DE: business travelers exempt (rare — most cities removed this)
Venice: students enrolled in local institutions exempt; business-trip guests exempt

diplomatic_government.description: Most US states respect federal diplomatic card + US govt/military official-business exemption.
examples: Texas: Hotel Occupancy Tax Exemption Certificate (Form 12-302)

disability_and_medical.description: Common European pattern — persons with disability + 1 accompanying carer exempt; patients + carers at medical facilities exempt.
examples: Common to France, Italy, Spain, Portugal (varies by municipality — needs per-city verification before seeding)

proposals_not_yet_enacted.status: Under consideration in Andalusia; no active tax yet
Planned for 2024, SCRAPPED after regional government change
Under review; could raise £240M+/yr. Framework being considered at national level for 2026+

proposals_not_yet_enacted.change: Visitor levy empowerment for local councils
proposals_not_yet_enacted.status: Announced by national government Nov 2025; implementation expected 2026+, likely not effective before 2027

notes: This file focuses on the RULES gap: 722 of 869 rated jurisdictions in our DB have ZERO active rules. Most entries below include at least one rule per finding.
All condition fields use the BookingContext allow-list (guest_age, stay_length_days, guest_type, property_type, guest_nationality) so they will pass the validator added in commit 2fd130b.
Some rules reference property_type values not in our taxonomy (e.g., PR `casino_hotel`). Flagged in notes.
Edinburgh entry under GB-SCT-EDI CORRECTS the prior entry filed under GB-SCT-EDH (which doesn't exist in DB). Prior rate was skipped by the seeder; this re-seeds under the right code.
